# Xuanqiang Angelo Huang's Blog

> Here I write and share about interesting topics I learn. Personal site of Xuanqiang Angelo Huang: blog posts and a large collection of computer-science university notes (ETH Zürich, University of Bologna).

Machine-readable alternatives: [RSS feed](https://flecart.github.io/index.xml), [sitemap](https://flecart.github.io/sitemap.xml).

## Posts

- [Through Humanity, Measures, and Impossibility](https://flecart.github.io/posts/2026-07-15-through-humanity-measures-and-impossibility/): We analyze briefly phenomena of social-scientification of computer science, of measuring historically human things, and the classical idea of limits of human reach, contrapposed with the longing of wa
- [On Norms and Instructions](https://flecart.github.io/posts/on-norms-and-instructions/): This short note explores the connection of rules to the language and the difference between precise and ambiguous rules. It analyzes briefly the concept of intentions and the legislative process, with
- [What Is Punishment for an AI Agent](https://flecart.github.io/posts/2026-06-06-what-is-punishment-for-an-ai-agent/): Taking some concepts of punishment from the Human sphere and applying it to AI Agents.
- [AI Social Norms](https://flecart.github.io/posts/2026-05-24-ai-social-norms/): What we shall expect from AI, and how as a society we will need to engage with this problem.
- [May 21th 2026](https://flecart.github.io/posts/2026-05-21-may-21th-2026/): Re-engineering the blog stack and some random thoughts on academia's publish-or-perish culture as a tragedy of the commons.
- [Optimizing Single-Core CPU software](https://flecart.github.io/posts/2025-10-21-optimizing-single-core-cpu-software/): Fully exploiting the abilities of modern hardware is hard. Commonly written software does not fully exploit all of the underlying hardware optimization abilities, and often needs specialized software 
- [Hello World](https://flecart.github.io/hello-world/): First blog post
